rulings.Case Title: TOM PISANI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION N.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PER STIPULATION, THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE SHIFTED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS WHEN DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES REMOVED AND CUT GASKETS IN PLAINTIFF'S PRESENCE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: TOM PISANI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER JUDGE GOLDSMITH'S AUGUST 26, 2008 ORDER. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALDINO SANI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CLAIMS FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PREMISES LIABILITY AND DENIED AS TO OTHER ISSUES. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE ON THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE AS IS THE AY DECLARATION AND BOTH CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. ON PUNITIVE DAMAGES, DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES DO NOT ADDRESS THAT ISSUE. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ABUID ARCHULETA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT A.H. VOSS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350(d). PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED TO CUTTING DEFENDANT'S PIPE AND DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVE THAT PIPE CONTAINED ASBESTOS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LORENZO LOTTA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE LELAND STANFORD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: BENNIE KENNEDY VS. ASBESTOS DEFEWNDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: The matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2008, Line 5. DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's testimony and plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories, in particular UMF 49 and 50. Plaintiff fails to present evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff provides no evidence for plaintiff's contention that the valves plaintiff worked on contained asbestos. (302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BENNIE KENNEDY VS. ASBESTOS DEFEWNDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: The matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2008, Line 5. DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted based on plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories 4 b, 5, 6 b, 7, 19 and 12 and 13. Defendant has demonstrated that plaintiff lacks evidence to support the contention that the valves plaintiff worked on contained asbestos manufactured or sold by defendant. Plaintiff's declaration submitted in opposition is ambiguous. Plaintiff describes "removing Buffalo Pumps, installed new ones and rebuilt them." Plaintiff then goes on to describe his work on "internal gaskets on Buffalo Pumps" but does not state whether the pumps were new and/or rebuilt. The question remains unanswered. What evidence does plaintiff have that the pumps he worked on contained asbestos for which Buffalo Pumps is responsible? (302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BENNIE KENNEDY VS. ASBESTOS DEFEWNDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: The matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2008, Line 5. DEFENDANT ROSENDAHL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories of Jan-31-2008 and plaintiff's deposition testimony of Jan-30-2008. The Kennedy Declaration is subject to a motion to strike under D'Amico. Plaintiff fails to offer any explanation for how it is he now recalls such details. The Ay declaration lacks the requisite foundational facts for the opinions expressed. (302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: EARL MOSEBY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 6, DEFENDANT AMCORD INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR, MOVING PARTY DISMISSED ON AUGUST 15, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN HARRINGTON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 16, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN HARRINGTON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: The matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2008, Line 7. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing based on the defense of the statute of limitations. CCP 361 applies and the Oregon statute of limitations, 30.907, establishes a 2 year limitations period that had expired when plaintiff brought suit in California. Cossman v. Daimlerchrysler Corp. (2003) 108 Cal. App. 4th 370. Defendant has demonstrated that plaintiff's kidney cancer is the result of plaintiff's exposure to asbestos UMF 11-15, as well as the testimony of Dr. Gotchall. (302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitation

rulings.Case Title: ROSE ABAD VS. BARBARA COHEN et al
rulings.Rulings: The matter on calendar for Thursday, October 16, 2008, Line 13. DEFENDANT BARBARA COHEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden of producing evidence. It appears that all parties share the kitchen, which is part of the unit of each. Defendant's notice of motion fails to set forth the issue to be adjudicated. (302/PJM)
